title: ConfigMaps, Secrets, Environment, Projection, Rotation, and Change Propagation · API Object Lens: K8s Secret Rotation Boundary
summary: A payment-free intake service mixes public config with credentials, assumes environment values rotate, and releases without proving how changed data reaches running Pods. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s secret rotation boundary increment in configuration and secret propagation contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design short-lived credentials, audience, rotation, revocation, redaction, audit, reload, failure, and recovery evidence across workload and external systems.
Trace mounted refresh latency, environment immutability, checksum or generated-name rollout triggers, readiness, and rollback for changed configuration.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Secret Rotation Boundary · Theory K8s Secret Rotation Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory release embeds a credential in Git, mixes immutable and rotating config, uses env projection where a file watch is required, and grants broad Secret reads. Separate creation, consumption, update propagation, encryption, RBAC, and rotation evidence. Competency probe: Design short-lived credentials, audience, rotation, revocation, redaction, audit, reload, failure, and recovery evidence across workload and external systems.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Secret Rotation Boundary contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release embeds a credential in Git, mixes immutable and rotating config, uses env projection where a file watch is required, and grants broad Secret reads. Separate creation, consumption, update propagation, encryption, RBAC, and rotation evidence. Competency probe: Design short-lived credentials, audience, rotation, revocation, redaction, audit, reload, failure, and recovery evidence across workload and external systems. A defensible solution applies K8s Secret Rotation Boundary, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to configuration and secret propagation contract.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-config-secrets-projection-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-config-secrets-projection-3
    artifact: config-secret-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s config secrets projection 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      public-config: immutable-configmap
      runtime-secret: projected-bound-source
      token-audience: intake-api
      environment-refresh: rollout-required
      mounted-refresh: observed
      redaction: enforced
    verify: kubectl get configmap status-config -o json && kubectl auth can-i get secrets --as system:serviceaccount:intake:status-api
    failure: Reject base64-as-encryption, static environment rotation, broad secret reads, subPath refresh claims, or values leaked through rendered output.
    repair: Separate config and secret authority, scope projections and audiences, trigger observable rollouts, rotate credentials, and verify redaction.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Replacing a Secret object proves every consumer stopped using the old credential. This fails the K8s Secret Rotation Boundary contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-secret-rotation-boundary-ea8lrf
  - competency: k8s-secret-rotation-boundary
    artifact: config-secret-policy.yaml
    decision: secret rotation boundary
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: withdraw the selected image digest after one node pulls it
    retained-proof: EndpointSlice DNS policy route and packet-path correlation
    case-key: ea8lrf
    change: |
      public-config: immutable-configmap
      runtime-secret: projected-bound-source
      token-audience: intake-api
      environment-refresh: rollout-required
      mounted-refresh: observed
      redaction: enforced
    verify: kubectl get configmap status-config -o json && kubectl auth can-i get secrets --as system:serviceaccount:intake:status-api
    failure: Reject base64-as-encryption, static environment rotation, broad secret reads, subPath refresh claims, or values leaked through rendered output.
    repair: Separate config and secret authority, scope projections and audiences, trigger observable rollouts, rotate credentials, and verify redaction.
